description: Copying a live .db file (especially in WAL mode) produces a backup that is inconsistent or missing recent commits.
body: Failure mode: a backup job runs cp/rsync on the database file while connections are open; in WAL mode committed data sits in the -wal file not yet checkpointed, so the copied main file is stale or torn and may fail to open. Detection signals: restored backups fail integrity_check or are missing recent writes; backup is a plain file copy; -wal/-shm not included. Do not copy a live database file. Safer fallback: use VACUUM INTO or the online backup API for a consistent single-file copy and verify every backup with PRAGMA integrity_check.
